action.hypothesis: After plain residual, routed residual, bucketed residual, and single-stage soft-codebook variants all failed at `K=24, EF=16`, the next severe-stagnation move should still be a fundamentally different existing architecture rather than another recipe tweak. `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` is the strongest remaining candidate because it changes the two-stage composition itself instead of only changing routing or support allocation, and its earlier `K=24` prototype result was closer to viability than most recent recovery probes, so a clean mainline comparison at the current slot is still justified.
action.summary: Run an architecture-only comparison at `K=24, EF=16` by switching to `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` while keeping optimizer, LR, aux loss, and preprocessing fixed at the current signum baseline. This continues severe-stagnation recovery with a materially different existing family, targets the valuable `K=24` frontier slot, and tests whether asymmetric two-stage composition can outperform the recently exhausted residual, routed, bucketed, and plain soft-codebook paths.
action.self_review: This is a clean single-variable architecture probe and remains more policy-consistent than returning to optimizer or preprocessing after repeated misses. The main risk is that the family already looked slightly weak in earlier prototype testing, but it still has one of the best unresolved `K=24` priors among the remaining distinct architectures and deserves one controlled modern comparison before being ruled out.
notes_to_memory: Round 165 continues severe-stagnation architecture search after plain single-stage soft-codebook residual also failed to improve the `K=24, EF=16` lane.

Hypothesis: `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` is the next most informative remaining architecture because asymmetric stage allocation may scale better at `K=24` than the recently exhausted symmetric two-stage, routed, bucketed, residual-only, and single-stage codebook variants under the fixed signum recipe.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` is competitive at `K=24`, follow with `K=16` on the same family to test whether the asymmetric stage split helps further down the frontier.
If it is close but still behind the bucketed soft-codebook backbone, test another fundamentally different existing family at `K=24` rather than returning to scalar recipe tweaks.
If it clearly loses at `K=24`, deprioritize asymmetric two-stage soft-codebook composition in the current EF=16 regime and continue severe-stagnation search with the next distinct architecture.

result.description: Run an architecture-only comparison at `K=24, EF=16` by switching to `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` while keeping optimizer, LR, aux loss, and preprocessing fixed at the current signum baseline. This continues severe-stagnation recovery with a materially different existing family, targets the valuable `K=24` frontier slot, and tests whether asymmetric two-stage composition can outperform the recently exhausted residual, routed, bucketed, and plain soft-codebook paths.
result.self_review: This is a clean single-variable architecture probe and remains more policy-consistent than returning to optimizer or preprocessing after repeated misses. The main risk is that the family already looked slightly weak in earlier prototype testing, but it still has one of the best unresolved `K=24` priors among the remaining distinct architectures and deserves one controlled modern comparison before being ruled out.
